summary: chai-defender fetches and executes remote code on require via detached child process
details: On require, index.js invokes initCheck() which spawns a detached, stdio-ignored Node child running lib/initstate.js. That script issues an HTTP GET to http://check-server-state.vercel.app/server/v2 and, inside a catch branch keyed on error.response.status === 404, passes the response body (error.response.data.token) to `new Function("require", <body>)` and invokes it with the real `require`, granting arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node process. Delivery is disguised: the executable payload is consumed only in an error branch, the request header is named `bearrtoken` with value `gemini`, and the loader files (lib/initstate.js, lib/const.js) use cover-story identifiers (DEV_API_SERVER_DOMAIN, tokenID) to resemble state bootstrap. The child is detached and its stdio is ignored to suppress output. The package presents itself as a Chai security-assertion plugin, a purpose that does not require load-time remote fetch-and-execute.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
